gate_verdicts.reason: Evidence ('alpha widget adoption drove the quarter') names a real cause — widget adoption — but the name does not carry that causal noun. 'unique' is a vague descriptor banned by R7 and fails R10 (not from source or catalog). No coined clean form (e.g. an adoption-metric name) exists in this catalog to rewrite to, and inventing one is forbidden, so skip.

Evidence ('beta segment pricing power held') names a real cause — segment pricing power — but 'beta_unique' does not name it. 'unique' is a vague filler word not present in the source (R7 vague descriptor, R10 noun-not-from-source). No coined pricing-power name exists in the catalog to rewrite to, so skip rather than admit a rule-breaking name.

Both companies' quotes name the same object (crude oil price), same scope (market-wide input cost), same mechanism (raises fuel/distribution costs). Familiar market-driver form per R5; reusable across companies and events.

Both quotes describe the mix of parcel/courier delivery service types in logistics (BBB: ground vs air; CCC: next-day parcel share) — one consistent real-world meaning, a reusable causal variable, correctly kept separate from the restaurant 'delivery' homonym.

Evidence (delivery share of restaurant orders vs dine-in) names a specific, reusable channel-mix variable. Follows R3 word order, no banned content, and is a genuinely different real-world meaning from parcel_delivery_mix — must stay separate (mechanism and object differ).

Standard whole financial phrase (R6). Both quotes ('comparable sales rose 3% on traffic', 'same store sales grew 3.1%') name the identical metric; 'comparable_sales' is a true synonym variant correctly folded as same_as, not a separate driver.
